title: Judge Strikes Down RFK Jr.’s Vaccine Policies in Blow to Trump’s Health Agenda
summary: Ruling on a lawsuit brought by several prominent medical organizations, a district court said the federal government had not based its decisions on science in limiting Covid shots and revising the childhood immunization schedule.

title: In Tense Meeting, Mehmet Oz Pressed Medical Societies on Trans Care for Teens
summary: Most groups defended their support for medical intervention. But the Society for Plastic Surgeons broke with the consensus.

title: No H.I.V. Aid Without More Access to Minerals: U.S. Ponders ‘Sticks’ Against Zambia
summary: A draft State Department memo outlines ways the Trump administration may ratchet up pressure on the African country by ending health support “on a massive scale.”

title: Cuba Ready to Accept Outside Investment, Top Official Says
summary: President Trump’s words came amid a nationwide blackout and as a top Cuban official said his country would move to open the economy to foreign investors.

title: Trump Administration Seeks to Remove Cuba’s President From Power During Negotiations
summary: The United States has told Cuba that for meaningful progress to be made in negotiations, President Miguel Díaz-Canel must step down, said people familiar with the talks.

title: With Threats and Claims of ‘Treason,’ Trump Pressures Media on the War
summary: A president who calls journalists the “enemy of the people” has accused news organizations of undermining a military effort that many Americans oppose.

title: Trump Claims an Ex-President Confided His Regrets on Iran. But Who?
summary: The New York Times reached out to people close to President Trump’s predecessors. They disputed Mr. Trump’s claims.

title: Fire on the U.S. Aircraft Carrier Gerald R. Ford Raged for Hours, Sailors Say
summary: The Ford is now entering its 10th month of deployment after arriving in the Middle East from the Caribbean.

title: Gregory Bovino to Retire From Border Patrol
summary: The outspoken border official drove the agency’s aggressive immigration policy in U.S. cities, including Minneapolis, until January.

title: Why Republicans are Changing Course on Immigration
summary: What did Speaker Mike Johnson mean when he talked about a “course correction” in the Trump administration’s deportation approach? Our White House correspondent Zolan Kanno-Youngs analyzes how the message around deportation is changing.

title: ICE Releases Columbia Protester Who Was Held for One Year
summary: Leqaa Kordia, 33, had been held in a Texas facility, where she said her health had declined. She was arrested during a 2024 protest and then detained by ICE in March 2025.

title: Best and Worst Moments From the 2026 Oscars
summary: There was a lot to take in, from Michael B. Jordan’s thrilling win to the perplexing “bum drum.”

title: How ‘Marty Supreme’ and Timothée Chalamet Went Home With No Oscars
summary: A few years ago, the indie studio A24 was luxuriating in Academy Awards. On Sunday it was shut out entirely.

title: For Once, the Oscars Got a Lot Right (Even the In Memoriam Segment)
summary: The ceremony figured out how to celebrate movies and the people who make them. It even understood Robert Redford’s place in American cinema.

title: War Isn’t the Only Thing on Trump’s Mind
summary: President Trump spoke to journalists for about 40 minutes before holding a news conference on Monday, offering a glimpse into his priorities.

title: Kennedy Center Board Votes to Close for 2-Year Renovation Project
summary: Ahead of the vote, President Trump criticized the center’s physical condition and previous financial management.

title: Airport Security Lines Grow as TSA Goes Unpaid in Partial Shutdown
summary: “There’s going to be a breaking point sooner or later,” one union official warned, with travelers at some airports being told to arrive three hours ahead of time.

title: S.E.C.’s Enforcement Chief Abruptly Resigns
summary: Margaret A. Ryan is leaving the agency just six months after joining it, a surprise given that most people hold the position for years.

title: Dinner and No Drinks: Restaurants Are Struggling as Americans Drink Less
summary: Traditionally a reliable revenue stream for restaurants, alcoholic drinks are down markedly — and the bottom line is, too.

title: In Deep-Red Idaho, a Republican Rift Over Schools and ‘Parental Choice’
summary: Does “choice” in Idaho mean vouchers for private-school tuition or publicly funded remote learning that has brought AP classes and advanced math to the state’s rural reaches?

title: My Mother’s Dying Wish Took Me on a Trip I’ll Never Forget
summary: What I thought was a burden was a tether across death’s divide.

title: 4 Are Dead, Including a Child, in a Fast-Moving Queens Fire
summary: The building in Flushing was engulfed in flames by the time firefighters arrived four minutes after a 911 call. Twelve people were injured, at least one critically.

title: Spaceflight Started 100 Years Ago in a Massachusetts Cabbage Patch
summary: Before humanity sent satellites, telescopes, humans and weapons into space, Robert Goddard experimented with the first liquid-fueled rocket on his aunt’s farm.

title: I Predicted the 2008 Financial Crisis. What Is Coming May Be Worse.
summary: Richard Bookstaber warns that what appears to be disparate forces adding risk to our economy are all part of one deeply connected system.

title: How Trump Destroyed the Aura of the Wartime President
summary: Even as he’s wrecking American institutions, he is revealing the limits of his cultural influence.

title: A Judge Strikes Down Kennedy’s Vaccine Policies
summary: Also, Trump raises the possibility of the U.S. “taking Cuba.” Here’s the latest at the end of Monday.

title: We Want to Hear From Parents and Doctors About Vaccines
summary: Medical groups and the Trump administration are in a battle over vaccine recommendations. What are your questions and concerns?

title: Live Nation Antitrust Case Resumes With Testimony About Springsteen Fees
summary: After the Justice Department announced a deal with the concert giant, the trial picked up after a week’s break, with a coalition of states leading the way.

title: Judy Pace, 83, Dies; Actress Brought Layers to Black Characters
summary: On the prime-time soap opera “Peyton Place,” she played one of TV’s first Black female antagonists. She was also a fixture in blaxploitation films.

title: What Displays Get Scrapped at America’s Parks? It Looks Like Anyone’s Guess.
summary: President Trump ordered officials to remove information deemed disparaging to the United States. A review of government documents shows little guidance and striking inconsistencies.

title: Hydropower Line From Quebec to Queens Could Power a Million N.Y.C. Homes
summary: A 339-mile buried transmission line is on schedule to bring clean electricity to New York City this spring.
